Title: Selective utilization of medicinal polysaccharides by human gut Bacteroides and Parabacteroides species

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Prebiotic polysaccharide utilization by gut bacteria; selective microbiome modulation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Polysaccharide utilization locus containing SusC-like transporter; SusD-like glycan-binding protein; GH26; GH5_7; CE and other CAZyme-related genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated when B. uniformis DA183 is grown on Dendrobium polysaccharides versus glucose
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated on Dendrobium polysaccharides in B. uniformis
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mixed locus components including outer membrane and secreted/periplasmic CAZyme-related proteins
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables highly specific utilization of Dendrobium polysaccharides by B. uniformis; deletion abolishes growth on DPs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics; CRISPR/Cas-based knockout of two PUL34 regions; growth profiling; sequence distribution analysis across species
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Minimal medium with Dendrobium polysaccharides (DPs_2) or other plant-derived mannans as sole carbon source; anaerobic culture at 37 °C
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GH26_BuDA183; GH5_7; SusC-like protein; SusD-like protein
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only B. uniformis among the tested 28 strains encoded a complete PUL34_Bu, matching its highly specific DPs utilization profile
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful genetic module for engineering gut Bacteroides/probiotics to enhance targeted utilization of medicinal glucomannan-type prebiotics
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PUL34_Bu was the most strongly induced locus during growth on Dendrobium polysaccharides. 2. Knockout of either a major induced region or the key SusC/SusD/GH26 region abolished growth on DPs. 3. Presence of a complete PUL34_Bu explains the selective DPs utilization phenotype of B. uniformis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports selective gut microbial growth and prebiotic response
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH26 glycoside hydrolase; bacterial Ig-like domain; GH26 catalytic domain; galactose-binding lectin/CBM-like region
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly upregulated in B. uniformis DA183 grown on DPs versus glucose
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High under DPs and plant-derived mannan conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for DPs/mannan utilization in PUL34_Bu
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted/exported enzyme with N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for utilization of Dendrobium polysaccharides and multiple plant-derived mannans; hydrolyzes mannan substrates with preference for glucomannan
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DPs, konjac glucomannan, guar gum galactomannan, carob galactomannan, ivory nut mannan as sole carbon sources; in vitro enzyme assays at 37 °C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic residues E415 and E504 are essential; CBM-like domain contributes to thermostability rather than catalytic efficiency; A363 differs from B. ovatus K149 and contributes partly to substrate preference
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value for engineering gut strains to consume medicinal glucomannans and related mannans selectively
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PUL34_Bu SusC-like protein; PUL34_Bu SusD-like protein
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes internal beta-1,4-mannosidic linkages in glucomannan and other plant-derived mannans via a retaining double-displacement mechanism
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Dendrobium polysaccharides; konjac glucomannan; guar gum galactomannan; carob galactomannan; ivory nut mannan; galactosyl mannobiose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; PUL knockout growth loss; recombinant protein expression and purification; DNS enzyme assays; catalytic-site mutagenesis; molecular docking; structural modeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH26 in PUL34_Bu was highly induced by Dendrobium polysaccharides. 2. Genetic deletion of the PUL34 segment containing GH26 abolished growth on DPs and other plant mannans. 3. Purified GH26 hydrolyzed six mannans, with highest activity toward glucomannan, and required catalytic residues E415 and E504.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports selective gut bacterial growth on glucomannan
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly upregulated in PUL34_Bu on DPs versus glucose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to DPs utilization as a strongly induced mannan-degrading enzyme in PUL34_Bu
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is transcriptomic annotation-based; no direct single-gene mutational or purified-enzyme validation reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary enzyme for gene stacking with GH26 in engineered mannan utilization modules
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted to cleave beta-1,4-mannan backbone of glucomannan
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH5_7 was one of the two significantly induced GHs in PUL34_Bu during DPs utilization. 2. Annotation indicates endo-beta-1,4-mannanase activity. 3. It is a likely accessory enzyme in B. uniformis glucomannan utilization.
